description: A candid black-and-white documentary photograph taken in an office/meeting room with a patterned carpet. On the left foreground stands an older man with short gray hair in a short-sleeved polo shirt and jeans with a belt, gesturing with both hands as he speaks, shown in profile. Behind him to the left is a flag on a pole and a large round DHS / 'U.S. DEPARTMENT OF HOMELAND SECURITY' seal mounted/leaning on the wall. To the right stand two people facing him: a man in a dark suit and tie whose face is digitally blurred, and a woman with long dark hair wearing a striped/checked blazer over a light top and dark pants, with a crossbody bag strap, listening. There is no overlaid headline, slogan, or designed text on the image; it is a plain candid photo (per the tweet, ICE Chicago meeting with Joe and Michelle Abraham, parents connected to the 'Midway Blitz' operation). The black-and-white treatment gives it a somber, documentary feel but it is not a designed graphic/meme. Intent: to humanize victims' families and ICE leadership and lend an emotional, sympathetic frame to the enforcement operation.
notable_text: Wall seal reads 'U.S. DEPARTMENT OF HOMELAND SECURITY'. No designed slogan overlay.
